Not the worst hotel I've stayed in, but close. The cleanliness was below average — our room was surface-level tidy but the bathroom grout was dark and the vent was caked with dust. We checked out the amenities but the gym had outdated equipment and the pool was barely warm. Had to ask twice for extra pillows and the service each time was slow and disinterested.

Booked this for a work trip expecting something decent. Housekeeping made the beds but left dust on the nightstands and old coffee cups from the previous guest, so the cleanliness wasn't great. Restaurant was overpriced for mediocre food and the fitness center closed early — amenities were a letdown. The front desk attendant seemed overwhelmed and the service on our billing issue was frustrating.

I've seen worse, but not recently. The bathroom was mostly clean but the exhaust fan was broken leaving everything damp and musty — the cleanliness needed work. The business center had outdated PCs that froze and the pool was cluttered with broken furniture, so the amenities disappointed us. Lisa at the front desk tried to help but the service overall lacked training; wrong charges appeared twice.

Stayed here with my partner for a long weekend. Our room had been vacuumed but corners were dusty and the bathroom caulking was yellowed — the cleanliness was surface-level at best. Tried the spa but only two overpriced treatments were available, and the hot tub was tepid — the amenities underwhelmed. Concierge gave us outdated restaurant recommendations, so the service wasn't helpful.

It was fine, I guess? Surfaces were wiped but the carpet had stains and the bathroom tiles were chipped, so the cleanliness left something to be desired. WiFi dropped every hour, the gym was cramped, and the breakfast buffet ran out of eggs by 8am — the amenities were frustrating. Front desk was cordial but couldn't resolve our noise complaint, so the service fell short.

Look, it wasn't terrible but it wasn't good either. Fresh linens but a cigarette burn on the blanket, chipped bathroom tiles — the cleanliness was inconsistent. Pool was functional but the deck was cracked, exercise room smelled of rubber — the amenities need investment. Housekeeping acknowledged requests but the service rarely followed through; asked for hangers three times.

Business trip, three nights. Room was acceptable at first glance but sticky drawer handles and a dusty vent cover told the real story — the cleanliness was lacking. Gift shop was nearly empty and the gym had no free weights, business center printer was jammed — the amenities felt neglected. Staff smiled but the service seemed like they were going through the motions.

My wife and I stayed here en route to the coast. Bathroom was cleaned to a basic standard but the showerhead was caked with limescale and the towels felt scratchy — the cleanliness was just okay. Restaurant served bland food with few veggie options and the pool was so chlorinated our eyes burned — the amenities missed the mark. Bellhop was friendly but the rest of the service was disinterested.

Meh. Not dirty exactly, but neglected is the word — scuffed walls, wobbly desk chair, tarnished bathroom fixtures. Wouldn't call the cleanliness acceptable for the rate. Check-in was slow and impersonal and the night staff never answered the phone either.

Honestly expected more for what we paid. Beds were made but floors hadn't been mopped and last night's hallway trash was still there. On-site cafe served passable coffee but breakfast tasted reheated, hot tub jets didn't work. Every interaction with staff felt transactional, no warmth whatsoever. Service was just meh.

Room had a persistent musty smell and the bathroom drain was slow — the cleanliness was not up to par. Pool was open but leaves floating everywhere and the gym equipment had torn padding, nobody seemed to care about upkeep. The amenities need serious attention. At least the lobby looked presentable.

Dust under the bed, water stains on the ceiling, and a bathroom fan that made a grinding noise all night. The cleanliness was a problem. Restaurant had limited hours and the fitness center was locked without explanation — the amenities were frustrating. Common areas were fine but the room itself was rough.

Sheets looked clean but the mattress sagged badly and the drain smell never went away — the cleanliness was questionable. We flagged it to the front desk and the service was responsive but they couldn't actually fix the problem. Night manager was unreachable. Points for trying, I suppose.

Floors mopped but the bathroom corners were grimy and the window sill had a thick layer of dust, so the cleanliness hadn't been thorough in a while. Check-in was painfully slow — had to repeat our info three times. The service at this hotel needs a serious overhaul.

Cobwebs in the ceiling corner and an unclean coffeemaker. The cleanliness was disappointing for what they charge. Staff at checkout were friendly enough and the service included an apology, but nobody addressed these issues during our three-night stay despite daily housekeeping. Too little too late.

Broken exhaust fan left condensation on every bathroom surface. The cleanliness suffered because of it. Concierge meant well but gave poor directions that added an hour to our day — the service was well-intentioned but unhelpful. We appreciated the effort though.

Pool was the only amenity worth using, though the lounge chairs were stained and the towel bin was always empty. Restaurant was forgettable, business center felt abandoned. We managed to enjoy ourselves despite the hotel, not because of it.
Undersized gym, squeaky equipment, limited spa menu at premium prices, WiFi that dropped during every video call. The amenities were afterthoughts rather than genuine offerings. Not worth the rate.
Mildew along the shower seal. Gritty floor tiles even after housekeeping. We reported it twice with no improvement. Just never felt properly clean the whole stay.

Front desk was curt about late checkout, bellhop seemed put out by extra bags. Evening staff were slightly better but the overall service was grudging rather than welcoming. Not the hospitality experience we were hoping for.
